rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into component implementations when binding implementation-level behaviors, and into component types when expressing port-level assumptions or guarantees based on visible features.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL structure by only referencing ports, subcomponents, and connections explicitly defined in the source model.
Place implementation-specific bindings in implementation annexes; place port-level contracts in type annexes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes use `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax and must be inserted as direct children of component types or implementations.
Use only visible port names and constants declared in the AADL model within AGREE expressions.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE variables.
Do not invent numeric bounds (e.g., x >= 0) not explicitly stated in the requirement.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
